---
title: "Statistics for Data Analytics"
author: "Anish Rao - 20066423"
date: "2025-05-08"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
---
# Question 3
## a) Compute the likelihood function (LF).
```{r}
x = c(3,1,4,0)
n = 7
likefunc = function(p) {
  prod(dbinom(x, size = n, prob = p))
}

likefunc(0.3)
```

## b) Adopt the appropriate conjugate prior to the parameter p.
We choose beta distribution

Beta(1,1)

```{r}
prior_a = 1
prior_b = 1
```

## c) Using (a) and (b), find the posterior distribution of p.
```{r}
sum_x = sum(x)
total_n = length(x)*n

post_a = prior_a + sum_x
post_b = prior_b + total_n - sum_x

cat("Posterior distribution Beta(", post_a, ",", post_b, ")")
```

## d) Compute the minimum Bayesian risk estimator of p.
```{r}
bayesian_risk = post_a/(post_a + post_b)

cat("Bayesian risk estimator: ", bayesian_risk)
```
